title: Flaky Fried Russian Meat Pirozhki
description: Quick-fix Russian dish featuring pink food colouring, lamination-puff for tender, mustardy bites.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine flour, cold butter, and lard or shortening. Use a pastry blender or your fingertips to work the ingredients together until they resemble coarse crumbs.
Gradually add ice-cold water, stirring with a fork until the dough starts to come together in a shaggy mass.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead a few times until it becomes smooth and pliable.
Wrap the dough in plastic and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Preheat the oil in a deep frying pan to 375°F.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces. Roll out each piece into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
In a small bowl, mix together the minced onion, chopped fresh dill, salt, and black pepper. Spoon a tablespoon of this mixture onto one half of each dough circle.
Top the onion mixture with a tablespoon of beef or pork mince and a dollop of Dijon mustard.
Brush the edges of the dough with the beaten egg and fold the other half of the dough over the filling, pressing the edges together to seal.
Use a pastry brush to apply a thin layer of water to the edges of the dough, then press the edges together again to seal the pirozhki.
Dust the tops of the pirozhki with a little extra flour, then use a sharp knife to cut a few slits in the top of each pirozhki.
Add the pirozhki to the hot oil and fry for 2-3 minutes on each side, until they are golden brown and puffed up.
Using a slotted spoon, remove the pirozhki from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain any excess oil.
